primary_accounting_issue: Whether hedge accounting can be applied to foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend receivables in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items by restricting hedge accounting to external party transactions and providing a narrow exception for certain intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Addresses foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items that are not fully eliminated on consolidation, which is the basis for the exception in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6
Clarifies that hedge accounting for net investments applies only to foreign exchange differences between functional currencies, confirming the restrictive approach to intragroup hedging

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses consolidation procedures and reporting dates but does not establish hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items
Deals with disclosure of interests in other entities and restrictions, not hedge accounting eligibility
Addresses related party disclosures and key management compensation, not hedge accounting treatment

treatment_families.family: General prohibition on intragroup hedge accounting in consolidated statements
Exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: General prohibition on intragroup hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 explicitly restricts hedge accounting to transactions with external parties and permits intragroup hedging only in separate financial statements, not consolidated financial statements, making this the primary applicable approach for intragroup dividend foreign exchange risk

approaches.label: Narrow exception for intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides a limited exception allowing hedge accounting for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items whose FX differences are not eliminated on consolidation, which could theoretically apply if the dividend receivable meets the monetary item criteria under IAS 21
